content:
## Purpose and Scope

This Digital Adaptation Kit (DAK) documents the Core Information Standard v2.0, a comprehensive framework for person-centred health and social care information sharing. The standard facilitates seamless data exchange across health and social care settings while maintaining privacy, dignity, and person-centred approaches to care delivery.

The Core Information Standard encompasses demographic data, clinical information, care planning, and social care elements essential for coordinated care delivery. It supports the NHS Long Term Plan's vision of integrated care and enables professionals to access complete, accurate information when and where it's needed.

## Document Overview

This DAK provides implementers with the necessary guidance to adopt and deploy the Core Information Standard effectively. It includes data element specifications, process workflows, decision support logic, and performance indicators to ensure consistent implementation across organisations.

The standard was developed using WRIST (Wonderful Ramsey Systems Information Specification Tool) and aligns with UK Core FHIR R4 profiles, SNOMED CT clinical terminology, and NHS Data Dictionary standards.
title: Introduction

content:
## Key Clinical Recommendations

The following evidence-based interventions are supported by the Core Information Standard:

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source | Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| Personalised Care Planning | Develop collaborative care plans with person and carers | NICE NG86 | Strong |
| Information Sharing Consent | Obtain explicit consent for information sharing across services | GDPR/DPA 2018 | Mandatory |
| Reasonable Adjustments | Record and communicate accessibility requirements | Equality Act 2010 | Legal |
| Risk Assessment | Document safeguarding concerns and risk mitigation strategies | Care Act 2014 | Strong |
| Medication Reconciliation | Maintain accurate medication lists across care transitions | NICE NG5 | Strong |
| Advanced Care Planning | Record preferences for end-of-life care and treatment decisions | NICE NG142 | Recommended |
| Structured Assessment | Use validated assessment tools for consistent evaluation | NICE Guidelines | Recommended |

## Implementation Guidance

These recommendations should be embedded within organisational workflows and supported by appropriate clinical decision support systems. Regular audit and review processes ensure compliance with evidence-based practice standards.
title: Health Interventions & Recommendations

content:
## Key User Personas

The Core Information Standard serves diverse users across health and social care settings:

| Persona ID | Title | Description | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.P1 | GP/Primary Care Clinician | Provides comprehensive primary healthcare | Clinical assessment, care coordination, referral management |
| CISV2.P2 | Hospital Consultant | Specialist medical practitioner in secondary care | Specialist clinical expertise, complex case management |
| CISV2.P3 | Community Nurse | Delivers nursing care in community settings | Community health assessment, medication management |
| CISV2.P4 | Social Worker | Assesses and coordinates social care services | Needs assessment, safeguarding, care planning |
| CISV2.P5 | Care Home Manager | Oversees residential care provision | Care quality management, staff coordination |
| CISV2.P6 | Mental Health Practitioner | Provides mental health assessment and treatment | Mental health assessment, risk management, therapeutic interventions |
| CISV2.P7 | Service User/Patient | Individual receiving care and support | Self-advocacy, care preferences communication |
| CISV2.P8 | Informal Carer | Family member/friend providing unpaid care | Care coordination, advocacy, emotional support |

**GP/Primary Care Clinician** serves as the primary healthcare coordinator, requiring comprehensive access to person demographics, medical history, and care plans. **Social Workers** focus on holistic needs assessment and safeguarding, requiring detailed social context and risk information. **Service Users** need access to their own information and ability to communicate preferences and goals.
title: Generic Personas

content:
## Scenario 1: Hospital Discharge with Complex Needs

**Narrative:** Eleanor, an 82-year-old with dementia and diabetes, is being discharged from hospital following a fall. The discharge coordinator needs to share comprehensive information with community services, GP practice, and family carers to ensure safe transition and ongoing support.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Primary Actors | Hospital discharge coordinator, community matron, GP, family carer |
| Information Required | Demographics, medical conditions, medications, care preferences, risk assessments |
| Key Processes | Discharge planning, referral coordination, medication reconciliation |
| Success Criteria | Safe discharge with coordinated follow-up care |

**Linked Personas:** Hospital Consultant (CISV2.P2), Community Nurse (CISV2.P3), GP (CISV2.P1), Informal Carer (CISV2.P8)

## Scenario 2: Mental Health Crisis Response

**Narrative:** James, a 25-year-old with bipolar disorder, presents to A&E during a mental health crisis. The crisis team needs immediate access to his mental health history, risk assessments, care plans, and emergency contacts to provide appropriate intervention.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Primary Actors | A&E clinician, mental health crisis team, GP |
| Information Required | Mental health history, current medications, risk factors, crisis plan |
| Key Processes | Crisis assessment, safety planning, care coordination |
| Success Criteria | Effective crisis intervention with appropriate follow-up |

**Linked Personas:** Mental Health Practitioner (CISV2.P6), Hospital Consultant (CISV2.P2), Service User (CISV2.P7)

## Scenario 3: Safeguarding Concern Investigation

**Narrative:** A care home reports concerns about potential neglect of Margaret, a 78-year-old resident with learning disabilities. Social services need to access comprehensive information about her needs, care arrangements, and support network to investigate and ensure her safety.

**Linked Personas:** Social Worker (CISV2.P4), Care Home Manager (CISV2.P5)
title: User Scenarios

content: The Core Information Standard supports three critical care coordination processes that ensure seamless information flow while maintaining person-centred approaches and regulatory compliance.

activities.objectives: Establish and maintain accurate demographic and contact information for care coordination
activities.title: Person Registration and Demographics Management

activities.objectives: Conduct comprehensive needs assessment and develop collaborative care plans with person involvement
activities.title: Assessment and Care Planning

activities.objectives: Manage consent preferences and facilitate appropriate information sharing across care settings
activities.title: Information Sharing and Consent Management

title: Business Processes & Workflows

content:
## Data Element Categories

The Core Information Standard defines structured data elements across key domains:

### Demographics and Identity

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE1 | Person first name | First name(s) including middle names | String | Yes | M |
| CISV2.DE2 | Person family name | Family name or surname | String | Yes | M |
| CISV2.DE3 | Date of birth | Person's date of birth | Date | Yes | M |
| CISV2.DE4 | NHS number | Unique NHS identifier | String | Required | R |
| CISV2.DE5 | Gender | Person's stated gender | String | Required | R |
| CISV2.DE6 | Address line 1 | Primary address line | String | Yes | M |
| CISV2.DE7 | Postcode | Address postcode | String | Required | R |

### Clinical Information

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE8 | Problem description | Active health conditions | Coded text | Required | R |
| CISV2.DE9 | Medication name | Current medications | Coded text | Required | R |
| CISV2.DE10 | Allergy causative agent | Known allergens | Coded text | Required | R |
| CISV2.DE11 | Risk assessment | Identified risks | Text | Required | R |

### Care Planning

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE12 | Care goals | Person's care objectives | Text | Required | R |
| CISV2.DE13 | Support needs | Required support interventions | Text | Required | R |
| CISV2.DE14 | Care preferences | Person's care choices | Text | Optional | O |

### Social Context

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE15 | Household composition | Living arrangements | Coded text | Required | R |
| CISV2.DE16 | Accommodation status | Housing type | Coded text | Required | R |
| CISV2.DE17 | Social circumstances | Broader social context | Text | Required | R |
title: Core Data Elements

content:
## Information Sharing Decision Logic

**Description:** Determines appropriate information sharing based on consent status, legal basis, and care context.

| Person Capacity | Consent Status | Care Context | Emergency | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Has capacity | Explicit consent given | Routine care | No | Share full information |
| Has capacity | Consent refused | Routine care | No | Share essential safety information only |
| Has capacity | No consent recorded | Routine care | No | Obtain consent before sharing |
| Lacks capacity | Best interests decision | Routine care | No | Share information in person's best interests |
| Any | Any | Any | Yes | Share information for immediate care |
| Has capacity | Explicit consent | Mental health care | No | Share according to consent scope |
| Under 16 | Parent/guardian consent | Child care | No | Share with parental consent |

## Risk Assessment Decision Logic

**Description:** Categorises risk levels and determines required interventions based on assessment findings.

| Safeguarding Concern | Self-harm Risk | Capacity Issues | Support Network | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| High | High | Lacks capacity | Poor | Immediate safeguarding referral |
| High | Low | Has capacity | Good | Safeguarding alert with monitoring |
| Low | High | Has capacity | Poor | Mental health crisis referral |
| Low | Low | Has capacity | Good | Standard care pathway |
| Any | Any | Lacks capacity | Any | Mental capacity assessment required |

## Care Planning Decision Logic

**Description:** Determines care planning approach based on person's needs, preferences, and circumstances.

| Dependency Level | Cognitive Function | Family Support | Resources Available | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| High | Impaired | Limited | Available | Formal care package |
| High | Good | Available | Limited | Family-led care with support |
| Medium | Good | Available | Available | Shared care approach |
| Low | Good | Available | Available | Self-directed care with monitoring |
title: Decision-Support Logic

content:
## Implementation Quality Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND1 | Demographics Completeness | Records with complete core demographics | All person records | By care setting | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND2 | Information Sharing Consent | Records with documented consent status | All active records | By organisation | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND3 | Care Plan Currency | Care plans reviewed within timeframe | All care plans | By specialty | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND4 | Risk Assessment Coverage | Records with current risk assessment | High-risk individuals | By risk category | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND5 | Medication Reconciliation | Accurate medication lists at transitions | Care transitions | By transition type | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND6 | Contact Information Currency | Records with current contact details | All active records | By age group | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND7 | Reasonable Adjustments | Records with accessibility requirements | Individuals requiring adjustments | By adjustment type | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND8 | Safeguarding Documentation | Complete safeguarding assessments | Safeguarding concerns raised | By concern type | Monthly |

## Care Quality Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND9 | Person-Centred Planning | Care plans developed with person involvement | All care plans | By care setting | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND10 | Information Continuity | Successful information transfers | Care transitions requiring information | By provider pair | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND11 | Emergency Information Access | Emergency contacts accessible when needed | Emergency presentations | By time of day | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND12 | Care Coordination Effectiveness | Planned interventions completed on time | All planned interventions | By intervention type | Monthly |
title: Indicators & Performance Metrics

content:
## Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.FR1 | Person Registration | System must capture and maintain core demographic information | High |
| CISV2.FR2 | Consent Management | System must record and enforce information sharing preferences | High |
| CISV2.FR3 | Care Plan Creation | System must support collaborative care planning with person involvement | High |
| CISV2.FR4 | Risk Assessment | System must capture and communicate risk information | High |
| CISV2.FR5 | Information Sharing | System must facilitate secure information exchange between providers | High |
| CISV2.FR6 | Clinical Documentation | System must support structured clinical information capture | Medium |
| CISV2.FR7 | Audit Trail | System must maintain comprehensive audit logs for information access | High |
| CISV2.FR8 | Search and Retrieval | System must enable efficient information search and retrieval | Medium |

## Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.NFR1 | Data Security | System must implement appropriate security controls for health information | High |
| CISV2.NFR2 | Performance | System must respond to queries within 3 seconds for 95% of requests | High |
| CISV2.NFR3 | Availability | System must maintain 99.5% uptime during business hours | High |
| CISV2.NFR4 | Scalability | System must support concurrent access by 1000+ users | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR5 | Interoperability | System must support FHIR R4 and HL7 v3 messaging standards | High |
| CISV2.NFR6 | Usability | System interface must be intuitive for healthcare professionals | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR7 | Compliance | System must comply with GDPR, Data Protection Act 2018, and Caldicott principles | High |
| CISV2.NFR8 | Backup and Recovery | System must provide automated backup with 4-hour recovery time objective | High |
title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements

content:
## Standards and Frameworks

1. NHS Digital. [NHS Data Model and Dictionary](https://datadictionary.nhs.uk/). NHS Digital, 2023.
2. HL7 FHIR. [UK Core Implementation Guide](https://simplifier.net/ukcore). HL7 UK, 2023.
3. SNOMED International. [SNOMED CT UK Edition](https://termbrowser.nhs.uk/). NHS Digital, 2023.
4. NICE. [NICE Guideline NG86: Dementia: assessment, management and support](https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng86). NICE, 2018.
5. NHS England. [Personalised Care Framework](https://www.england.nhs.uk/personalisedcare/). NHS England, 2022.

## Legislation and Policy

6. UK Government. [Data Protection Act 2018](https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2018/12/contents). HMSO, 2018.
7. UK Government. [Equality Act 2010](https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2010/15/contents). HMSO, 2010.
8. UK Government. [Care Act 2014](https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2014/23/contents). HMSO, 2014.
9. UK Government. [Mental Capacity Act 2005](https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2005/9/contents). HMSO, 2005.
10. NHS Digital. [Information Governance Toolkit](https://www.dsptoolkit.nhs.uk/). NHS Digital, 2023.

## Professional Standards

11. Professional Record Standards Body. [Core Information Standard](https://theprsb.org/standards/coreinformationstandard/). PRSB, 2023.
12. Royal College of General Practitioners. [Good Practice Guidelines for GP Electronic Patient Records](https://www.rcgp.org.uk/policy/rcgp-policy-areas/health-informatics). RCGP, 2022.
13. Care Quality Commission. [How CQC Regulates: NHS Trusts](https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-providers/nhs-trusts). CQC, 2022.

## Implementation Guidance

14. NHS Digital. [FHIR Implementation Guide](https://digital.nhs.uk/services/fhir-apis). NHS Digital, 2023.
15. INTEROPen. [Care Connect Profiles](https://fhir.hl7.org.uk/). INTEROPen, 2023.
title: References
